text.title: Continuous Accessibility Automation Pipeline
text.summary: 5-stage continuous automation workflow, human-AI responsibility split, and implementation rules for regression prevention.

list.item: 1. AI-Powered Automated Scan: axe-playwright + WAVE + Lighthouse; baseline report with prioritized issues; false positives filtered
2. AI Issue Extraction: developer-friendly context; code snippets auto-extracted; visual annotations on screenshots; WCAG criterion reference
3. AI Fix Suggestions: code recommendations ranked by impact; multiple approaches; best practice guidance
4. Human Review: validation of AI suggestions; edge case identification; context-specific decisions; human-in-loop checkpoint before developer tickets
5. Continuous AI Monitoring: regression prevention on every deploy; new page scanning; trend analysis and reporting
AI handles: repetitive scanning, code extraction, report formatting, WCAG Q&A chatbot, draft alt text, priority ranking by traffic, regression testing automation
Humans handle: empathy in content decisions, meaningful alt text judgment, user testing validation, strategic fix decisions, edge cases, final AI output review, accessibility culture

rule.statement: Set a WCAG 2.2 AA baseline on the current codebase before enabling CI gates — gate on regressions, not the full backlog.
rule.rationale: Gating on the full existing backlog blocks all deploys immediately. Gating on regressions prevents new violations while allowing the team to fix the backlog incrementally.

rule.statement: Separate the automated backlog (AI-found) from the manual backlog (human-found) without merging unless source is tagged.
rule.rationale: Mixing sources obscures coverage gaps. An all-green automated backlog may still have significant undetected manual issues.

rule.statement: Track false positive rate monthly — if AI filter marks real issues as false positives, retune the filter rules.
rule.rationale: AI false-positive filters are not static. As the codebase evolves, the filter rules may exclude genuine violations. Monthly review prevents drift.

text.reference: CI gate: axe scan, baseline diff, fail on new Critical violations
Haiku prompt for scan result triage with VPAT section generation
